message:
test(fuzz): add raptorq encode→decode metamorphic round-trip target (resolves hporx)

Adds fuzz_raptorq_roundtrip to exercise the encoder/decoder symmetry
property otherwise covered only by hand-written unit tests:

  for any payload P in [1, max_object_size], the K systematic source
  symbols emitted by RaptorQEncoder, fed to RaptorQDecoder in any
  arrival order, reconstruct P byte-for-byte.

The target also asserts encoder structural invariants (source ESIs in
0..K-1 at systematic positions, exact symbol_size width) and supports
substituting one source symbol with a repair to drive the LT-equation
path. Falls back to additional repair symbols if the K-symbol prefix
fails to decode, only panicking when even K + R fail — that is a real
RFC 6330 violation rather than a config-induced false positive.

Complements raptorq_decode_bounds (decoder-only adversarial input) and
raptorq_envelope_decrypt (AEAD wrapper), neither of which probe
encoder→decoder identity.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dcgw.2","title":"fcp.grafana: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

Commit a0ff0b44.","created_at":"2026-03-09T03:15:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dcgw.3","title":"TEST: fcp.grafana Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dcgw.4","title":"E2E: fcp.grafana Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-dcww0","title":"TEST-GOOGLE-PEOPLE: reject unsafe base_url overrides","description":"Apply the real-service HTTP allowlist guard posture to the Google People connector. GooglePeopleConfig currently accepts a configure-supplied base_url directly, while GooglePeopleClient uses it to construct authenticated People API requests. Add fail-closed validation and regression tests for host pinning, localhost test allowance, and userinfo/query/fragment rejection, matching the existing Google Docs/Sheets/Drive/Calendar connector pattern.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:06:17.706875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:06:21.737581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ddc","title":"fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution","description":"# fcp.terraform: Module management and dependency resolution (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide safe visibility into Terraform module structure so agents can:
- inspect required providers/modules
- analyze dependency graph
- prepare plans with correct initialization

## Scope
- Run `terraform init` in a controlled, non-interactive mode (may download modules/providers).
- List required providers and versions.
- Enumerate modules and sources.
- Produce a canonical `ModuleGraph` summary.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.modules.read`
- `terraform.init` (dangerous-ish if it downloads content; policy-gated)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace
  - restrict process execution
- NetworkConstraints default deny unless module registries are explicitly allowed.
- No automatic downloads unless policy explicitly permits.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `terraform.providers.list`
  - `terraform.modules.graph`
  - Optional: `terraform.init`
- Canonical output type:
  - `ModuleGraph`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests with fixtures for:
  - module parsing
  - provider list parsing
  - policy denial of init without explicit capability

## Acceptance criteria
- Module graph summaries are deterministic and safe-by-default.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-dgzko","title":"[AUDIT-reality-check][MEDIUM] AGENTS.md performance targets disagree with README Performance Targets","description":"AGENTS.md at line 405 states the connector performance targets as: Cold start < 50ms, Request latency overhead < 1ms, Memory footprint < 10MB idle, Binary size < 20MB (compressed). README.md at line 1333 (Performance Targets) states: Cold start (connector activate) < 100ms / < 500ms (p50/p99). The README has been updated to more realistic / operational targets; AGENTS.md still carries the aspirational spec. Further: README has no memory-footprint or binary-size target at all, so those AGENTS.md numbers are orphaned claims without doc-level acknowledgment. Impact: agents reading AGENTS.md chase a 2x-stricter SLO than the project actually ships to; no harness proves either target. Suggested fix: (1) reconcile — either update AGENTS.md to match README's p50/p99 formulation, or update README with the stricter AGENTS.md targets IF there is a benchmark harness that proves them; (2) add a connector-activate benchmark harness (likely in crates/fcp-host/benches) that measures p50/p99 cold start against a reference connector; (3) land a binary-size check in CI that rejects a compressed fwc binary larger than the declared target. This is a doc-reality-drift bead, not a code bug, but resolving it sets an honest SLO the project can defend.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:37:44.575160Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:51:53.637019Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:51:53.636566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2852,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-dgzko","author":"jemanuel","text":"Reconciled AGENTS.md Performance Targets table with README p50/p99 canonical formulation (commit 6f7552d2). Dropped the orphaned <20MB binary-size target (no CI enforcement found in .github or scripts, no bench harness). Added a link back to README as the canonical source.","created_at":"2026-04-24T20:51:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Freeze the stable CLI contract schema for operator truth surfaces","description":"Background: the remaining CLI reach-through problem starts with contract shape. Until the project freezes one explicit operator-facing contract for readiness, show, health, and provenance-bearing status, later work will keep binding `fwc` to internal crate layout instead of to durable interfaces.

Scope: define the stable host or mesh-backed contract schema that `fwc` should consume for operator-critical truth surfaces. Cover machine-readable field names, provenance taxonomy, evidence-handle references, degraded/fallback markers, and the subset of human-facing summaries that must remain stable enough for operator tooling and docs.

Acceptance: the repo has a concrete contract fixture set for operator truth surfaces, with explicit fields for offline, node-local, mesh-backed, degraded, and fallback-derived answers. Future CLI work should be able to depend on these fixtures instead of re-reading internal implementation code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.216770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-03T21:01:41.870357Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T21:01:41.870180Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.216770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2410,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group contract note: these beads together define, adopt, and prove the new operator contract.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Move host and CLI implementations onto the frozen operator contracts","description":"Background: contract fixtures only matter if the host and CLI actually adopt them. Otherwise the repo still carries a truthful schema alongside a separate reach-through implementation.

Scope: implement the provider side and consumer side of the stable CLI contracts. Replace the remaining internal reach-through call sites with explicit contract fetches, ensure the host or mesh layer populates provenance and evidence fields from the real owners, and remove the most direct semantic-bucket dependencies from `fwc`.

Acceptance: `fwc` resolves operator-critical truth through explicit contracts rather than by reaching into semantic buckets, and regression fixtures make it obvious when the contract/provider/consumer relationship drifts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.800516Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:01:54.668946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:01:54.668359Z","close_reason":"Verified adoption layer with rch cargo check --all-targets and rch cargo test --test cual_integration operator_truth_fixture -- --nocapture; proof-layer follow-up remains in hoogu.3.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","fwc","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:52.800516Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:40.798497Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2411,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.2","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group contract note: these beads together define, adopt, and prove the new operator contract. Keep them tied to fixture-backed schemas, nearby unit or snapshot coverage, host-backed integration checks where applicable, redaction-safe structured logs, and explicit `rch` rerun commands so downstream live-truth, operator-evidence, and docs work can reuse the contract instead of re-deriving it.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:11Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.3","title":"[FCP3/P6.1] Add fixture and snapshot proof for the new CLI contracts","description":"Background: once the new contracts are adopted, the repo still needs a durable proof layer so future contributors can see whether a change preserved semantics or only preserved passing tests.

Scope: add machine-readable fixtures, output snapshots, and targeted regression checks for the new CLI contracts. Cover both structured output and the key human-facing summaries so that field names, provenance semantics, and evidence handles stay auditable.

Acceptance: contract drift becomes mechanically visible through fixtures and snapshots, and the resulting artifacts are reusable by later acceptance, docs, and cutover-proof beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:53.363815Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:12.091509Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:12.091212Z","close_reason":"Added 5 new operator truth fixtures (doctor, config, auth, history, readiness), doubling coverage to 10 answer classes. Keep them tied to fixture-backed schemas, nearby unit or snapshot coverage, host-backed integration checks where applicable, redaction-safe structured logs, and explicit `rch` rerun commands so downstream live-truth, operator-evidence, and docs work can reuse the contract instead of re-deriving it.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:11Z"},{"id":2453,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak claiming. Starting with exploration of existing CLI contract surfaces and identifying which outputs need fixture/snapshot coverage.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:04:07Z"},{"id":2454,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"SandyOak completed fixture additions. Added 5 new operator truth fixtures to fixture_matrix.json covering previously uncovered answer classes:
1. offline_doctor_manifest_checks: Offline doctor diagnostics from workspace manifests
2. node_local_config_get: Node-local config retrieval with schema evidence
3. node_local_auth_verify: Auth verification with credential validity (redaction-safe)
4. mesh_backed_history_with_audit_trail: History with audit chain evidence handles
5. degraded_readiness_probe: Degraded readiness with component health breakdown

Total fixtures: 10 (was 5). All 42 integration tests pass including fixture validation and operator truth contract tests. Also fixed truth_matrix_simulate_support assertion to accept contract evolution (known/unknown both valid).","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:01Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hscro","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-oauth refresh scope-subset check breaks providers that only return scope on refresh","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/token.rs:260-288 (commit 6077aa9a) — OAuthTokens::update_from_response treats ANY refresh response with scope != subset of self.scopes as InvalidTokenResponse. But self.scopes is empty whenever the initial token_response omitted 'scope' (extremely common — many providers including Google return scope on neither initial response nor refresh, others return only on refresh). When the refresh response happens to echo back granted scope as 'read write' but self.scopes is [], refreshed_scopes_are_subset(&[], &['read','write']) returns false (empty HashSet contains nothing), so the refresh fails with 'refresh response expanded granted scopes'. Suggested fix: treat self.scopes.is_empty() as 'unbounded grant' (accept any refreshed scope string) OR record the ORIGINAL requested scopes from authorize() and compare against those. Reproduction: construct OAuthTokens from TokenResponse{ scope: None, .. }, then update_from_response(TokenResponse{ scope: Some('read'), .. }). Expected: success, token updated. Actual: InvalidTokenResponse. Reviewer: cc_1.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:46:24.116837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:21:29.766244Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:21:29.765765Z","close_reason":"Accept refresh scopes when initial token omitted scope","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hwbx8","title":"[REVIEW-A9][MEDIUM] crates/fcp-async-core/src/lib.rs:1037: unbounded mpsc send can succeed after receiver close race","description":"File: crates/fcp-async-core/src/lib.rs:1037-1042, 1063-1065

Repro: use `channel::mpsc::unbounded_channel()`, coordinate one thread in `UnboundedSender::send()` after it observes `closed == false`, then call `UnboundedReceiver::close()` on another thread before the sender reaches `push()`. Because `send()` checks `closed` and enqueues in separate steps, the send can return `Ok(())` after close and publish a value into a channel that was already closed.

Suggested fix: make close-state + queue mutation atomic from the wrapper perspective (for example store both under one mutex, or recheck `closed` while holding the queue lock) and add a regression that forces the interleaving so post-close sends fail deterministically.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:36:25.210552Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:37.846216Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:37.846050Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 572d7318: serialize unbounded mpsc close with send and cover the close race","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-hwo","title":"fcp.linear - Linear Issue Tracking Connector","description":"# fcp.linear - Linear Issue Tracking Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Linear connector for issue/project automation (and optional bidirectional Beads sync) with deterministic, idempotent, auditable behavior.

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 97/100** — Critical Tier 1 connector.

**Why Critical**: Linear is the dominant issue tracker for modern engineering teams. Direct integration enables seamless human↔agent task handoff and creates the bridge between "human project management" and "agent task execution." This is the task coordination backbone.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Strategic Investment (High Value + Moderate Effort)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,
    ConnectorArchetype::Bidirectional
]
```
- **Operational**: GraphQL CRUD operations
- **Streaming**: Webhook event ingestion
- **Bidirectional**: If Beads↔Linear sync implemented

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Required for bidirectional sync and webhook dedupe
- State tracked: `{ sync_cursor: Option<SyncToken>, webhook_event_ids: Vec<EventId> }`
- Lease required for writer fencing during sync operations

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI recommended**: External API connector with OAuth credentials
- Memory isolation prevents token extraction
- Cross-platform consistent behavior

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    host_allow: vec\![
        "api.linear.app".into(),
        "linear.app".into(),
    ],
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
Issue tracking data handling:
```rust
// Webhook events are externally sourced
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// Issue content may contain user-supplied data
issue_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED;

// If issues contain URLs:
if issue.contains_urls {
    issue_content.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `linear.issues.read` | Safe | Read-only listing |
| `linear.comments.read` | Safe | Read-only data |
| `linear.projects.read` | Safe | Read-only data |
| `linear.issues.write` | Risky | Creates/modifies work items |
| `linear.comments.write` | Risky | Public communication |
| `linear.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Inbound vector config |
| `linear.beads.sync` | Dangerous | Bidirectional data sync |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Issue Management
- Full issue CRUD with metadata (priority, labels, assignees)
- Comment threads and discussions
- Label and status automation
- Team workload visibility
- Sprint/cycle management

### Project & Roadmap
- Project and roadmap access
- Milestone tracking
- Team capacity planning
- Cross-project dependency tracking

### Webhook Events
- Real-time event processing for automation triggers
- Status change notifications
- Assignment alerts

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Create a Linear issue for this bug I found"
"Whats blocking the current sprint?"
"Assign this task to the frontend team"
"Update issue status when PR is merged"
"Generate weekly sprint summary"
"Auto-triage incoming issues using BV algorithms"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### BV (Beads Viewer) — Bidirectional Sync
**This is the killer feature.**
- Linear issues ↔ Beads (agent-native tasks)
- Sync status, priority, and dependencies bidirectionally
- Use BV's graph algorithms for Linear triage:
  - PageRank for issue importance
  - Critical path analysis
  - Blocker detection

### Agent Mail Integration
- Auto-assign issues based on file reservations
- Route issue updates as mail notifications
- Thread Linear comments with agent discussions

### NTM (Neural Task Manager)
- Spawn agents for Linear issues automatically
- Break down Linear epics into agent-executable subtasks
- Report progress back to Linear

### CASS (Context-Aware Semantic Search)
- Link code sessions to Linear issues
- Semantic search across issue history
- Learn from resolution patterns

### UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)
- Create Linear issues from UBS findings automatically
- Link bug reports to specific code locations
- Track fix status through issue lifecycle

---

## Automation Unlocks

### Auto-Triage Pipeline
```
UBS finding → Create Linear issue → BV ranks priority → Agent Mail assigns → Agent works → PR links back
```

### Implementation Plan Generation
```
Linear issue description → Agent generates implementation plan → Creates subtasks → Links to codebase
```

### Status Sync
```
Git commit → Linked issue → Update Linear status → Notify stakeholders
```

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **GraphQL API** with full query/mutation support
- **Subscription support** for real-time updates (optional)
- **Webhook handler** for event-driven automation

### Recommended Crates
- `graphql_client` or `cynic` — GraphQL client
- `reqwest` — HTTP transport
- `serde_json` — JSON handling
- `tokio` — Async runtime

### Bidirectional Beads Sync Protocol
If enabled, this is a high-risk side effect requiring:
- Explicit capability token
- OperationIntent/Receipt to avoid duplicate writes
- AuditEvents for every sync operation
- Stable reason codes on denials
- Conflict resolution strategy (last-write-wins or manual)

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:work:linear:<workspace>` | Full workspace access | All CRUD operations |
| `z:project:<name>:linear` | Project-scoped access | Issues in specific project |
| `z:work:linear:readonly` | Read-only monitoring | List/get only |

### Single-Zone Binding
- One connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId`
- Cross-workspace sync requires separate instances
- Beads sync is per-zone; cannot sync across workspaces

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — GraphQL CRUD operations
- `streaming` — Webhook/event ingestion (if enabled)
- `bidirectional` — If Beads↔Linear sync implemented

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.linear.app:443`
- `linear.app:443` (OAuth flows)

### Credential Injection
- API key via `CredentialId` (simpler, personal use)
- OAuth token via `CredentialId` (team/workspace access)
- Never write credentials to disk

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `linear.issues.read` — List, get, search issues
- `linear.comments.read` — Read issue comments
- `linear.projects.read` — List projects, roadmaps
- `linear.teams.read` — Team information
- `linear.users.read` — User profiles

### Write Operations
- `linear.issues.write` — Create, update, move, assign
- `linear.comments.write` — Create, update, delete comments
- `linear.projects.write` — Modify project settings
- `linear.labels.write` — Manage labels

### Dangerous Operations
- `linear.webhooks.manage` — Webhook configuration
- `linear.beads.sync` — Bidirectional Beads sync (if enabled)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### Issue Operations
- `issue.create` — Create new issue
- `issue.update` — Update issue fields
- `issue.get` — Get issue by ID
- `issue.list` — List issues with filters
- `issue.search` — Full-text search
- `issue.move` — Move between projects/teams
- `issue.assign` — Assign to user
- `issue.relations` — Manage issue relationships

### Comment Operations
- `comment.create` — Add comment to issue
- `comment.update` — Edit comment
- `comment.delete` — Remove comment
- `comment.list` — List issue comments

### Project/Cycle Operations
- `project.list` — List projects
- `project.get` — Get project details
- `cycle.list` — List sprints/cycles
- `cycle.get` — Get cycle details

### Optional: Beads Sync
- `beads.sync.pull` — Import Linear issues to Beads
- `beads.sync.push` — Export Beads to Linear
- `beads.sync.bidirectional` — Full two-way sync

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- GraphQL query construction
- Response parsing
- Sync conflict resolution logic
- Rate limit handling

### Integration Tests
- wiremock-based GraphQL simulation
- Webhook event simulation
- Bidirectional sync scenarios

### E2E Compliance
- Validates default deny + NetworkConstraints
- Verifies receipts for write operations
- Confirms audit trail for sync operations

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- Deterministic tests; no live Linear calls in unit tests
- GraphQL operations work correctly
- Webhook events processed reliably (if enabled)
- Beads sync maintains data integrity (if enabled)
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements
